business_problem: The Choctaw Nation—a fully functioning sovereign government and the third-largest federally recognized US tribe with 200,000+ members, 12,000 employees, and operations spanning 22 casinos, 17 travel plazas, 32 medical facilities, ranching, and 150 government-style programs—was running a highly customized end-of-life JD Edwards implementation. Procurement and HR processes lacked integration, leading to manual data entry errors, multiple payments for the same invoice, and difficulty managing Certificate of Degree of Indian Blood (CDIB) hiring requirements.
solution_summary: Choctaw Nation of Oklahoma modernized its complex multi-business operation (22 casinos, 32 medical facilities, 60K acres of ranching, 12K employees, 200K tribal members served) by replacing a customized legacy JD Edwards system with Oracle Cloud HCM and ERP. The cloud platform addressed specific tribal sovereignty requirements, including CDIB (Certificate of Degree of Indian Blood) identity tracking for preferential hiring, reduced manual HR and payroll processes by 80% in the first month, and provided real-time financial visibility for aggressive expansion and procurement control.
business_outcome: Choctaw Nation achieved one version of the truth across HR and financials, eliminated 25% of manual steps, reduced manual HR and payroll processes by more than 80% in the first month, launched new self-service capabilities for employees and managers, and revamped recruiting to identify CDIB-eligible tribal members. The new system enabled the Nation to continue paying all employees during the 2020 casino closures—something the old system could not have supported.
metrics: 22 casino properties, 17 travel plazas, 32 medical facilities
More than 80% reduction in manual HR and payroll processes in the first month
architecture_clues: Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications adopted as integrated stack
Oracle Taleo (part of Oracle Cloud HCM) for CDIB-aware recruiting
evidence_quotes: Since implementing Oracle Cloud HCM and ERP, Choctaw Nation has eliminated 25% of manual steps and realigned processes to track the cloud application’s built-in best practices.
The organization reduced manual HR and payroll processes by more than 80% in the first month.
At the beginning of the pandemic, in spring of 2020, we had to close the casinos, yet we were able to continue to pay all of our employees while we were closed.

solution_components.name: Oracle Cloud HCM
solution_components.role: Manages 12K employee HR workflows including CDIB identity verification for tribal preference hiring, self-service benefits, payroll, and compliance with tribal sovereignty requirements.

solution_components.name: Oracle Taleo
solution_components.role: Specialized recruiting module enabling identification and recruitment of tribal members with CDIB documentation.

solution_components.name: Oracle Cloud ERP
solution_components.role: Consolidates finance and procurement from legacy systems; standardizes approval routing, purchase-order processing, and accounts payable for 22 casinos and 60K acres of operations.

solution_components.name: Oracle Consulting
solution_components.role: Provided implementation services to overcome adoption roadblocks and manage complex business transformation across diverse tribal operations.

data_flow: Employee records, CDIB identity data, and payroll information flow into Cloud HCM; procurement requisitions and purchase orders flow into Cloud ERP; both systems share unified financial and HR data through integrated platform.
